text_list: The foghorn's deafening, guttural roar vibrates deep within my chest.
A black-and-white background emerges from the darkness: the massive silhouette of a lighthouse, stretching into the dirty sky, and a heavy oak door.
The horn fades, replaced by the incessant, furious roar of the surf and the distant, angry cries of seagulls.
I stood at the threshold, my hands gripping the handles of my heavy suitcases in a death grip—my fingers had long since gone numb from the cold.
Entering main hall, I noticed an old man sitting at an old, shabby sofa.
The sofa was all in tatters; the old fabric, once soft and slightly rough, now looked like the wool of a sheep that had just been sheared.
His skin resembled the tanned leather of an old saddle - darkened by the constant sun, deeply cut with wrinkles and cracks.
He looked at me with slightly narrowed eyes, able to spot a petrel in the pitch darkness.

greetings.name: Old man
text_list: -Howdy, kid! Look at you, they sent a helper after all, the earth hasn't forgotten shht!
I am Robert Muirhead, the senior keeper of this lighthouse.
-What's your name, boy?

name_boy.name: Moore
text_list: -My name is Joseph Moore, sir.

hello.name: Old man
text_list: -Well, why are you standing on the threshold like an unhewn post, letting the wind shake you?
-Come on, drag your things to the dryer before the salt gets into your ports!

okay....name: Moore
text_list: -I'll be right back, sir.
The old man just grinned, muttering something into his beard as I turned around.
I picked up my heavy suitcases and headed down the dark corridor toward the barracks.

barracks_obedient.name: Moore
text_list: The barracks room was small, cold, and smelled heavily of mold.
I carefully unpacked my prompter clothes, hanging them near the small stove to dry.
At the bottom of my bag, my hand brushed against my old iron lighter. A small reminder of the mainland.
I lay down on the hard straw mattress, staring at the wooden ceiling until sleep finally took over.

talkback.name: Moore
text_list: -I just got off the boat, sir. I need a moment to catch my breath.
Muirhead's eyes narrowed, his face darkening with instant irritation.
-Catch your breath?! You came here to work, not to lounge around, you arrogant whelp!
-Drop your bags in the barracks and get out of my sight. Pronto!

barracks_angry.name: Moore
text_list: I slammed the barracks door behind me, trembling with pure rage.
The room was disgusting. Damp, freezing, with two narrow cots that looked more like coffins.
Too exhausted to unpack or even take off my wet coat, I collapsed onto the straw mattress.
My eyes closed heavily as the sound of the crashing waves blurred into a dark, restless dream...

rude_awakening.name: Muirhead
text_list: BANG!
A heavy boot violently kicked the frame of my iron cot, shaking me awake in pitch darkness.
Muirhead was standing over me, holding a leaking lantern, his face twisted in disgust.
'-Get your boots off the sheets, you filthy animal! Sleeping in your wet gear? You're rotting my goddamn bunks!-'
'-Strip those clothes to the dryer now, or you'll sleep on the stone floor!-'

choices.text: Strip clothes and try to sleep again

morning_start.name: Moore
text_list: BRRRRRRRRRRRRHN!
The morning arrived with the deafening, structural shake of the lighthouse foghorn.
My head felt like it was split in half. The air in the room was freezing.
It was time to face my first actual day of work. I put on my stiff, salt-crusted boots and walked out.

kitchen_first_entry.name: Moore
text_list: The kitchen was hot and suffocating. The smell of frying grease, stale tobacco, and old fish hit my nose.
Muirhead was already sitting at a stained wooden table, a grease-splattered bottle of some murky alcohol standing before him.
He filled two tin cups to the brim, the heavy metal clinking against the wood.

the_first_toast.name: Muirhead
text_list: The old man raised his cup, his piercing, bloodshot eyes locking directly onto mine. His voice boomed, drowning out the distant wind outside:
'-Should death drag us into the swirling vortex tomorrow, let the Almighty hear the prayers of lost souls above the roar of the waves!'

drink_toast.name: Moore
text_list: I took the cup and swallowed the liquid. It tasted absolutely vile, like burning kerosene and rot. I choked back a cough.
Muirhead grinned, looking satisfied that his ritual wasn't interrupted.

refuse_toast.name: Muirhead
text_list: I left my cup untouched on the table.
Muirhead's grin vanished instantly. He slammed his fist down, making the tin cups rattle.
'-You dare look down on my hospitality, boy?! You think you're too good for a sailor's prayer?!'

work_discussion.name: Moore
text_list: Wanting to change the subject before things got worse, I leaned forward.
'-I'm ready to take the night watch at the lantern tonight, sir.-'
Muirhead cut me off immediately, his face hardening.
'-The Light is MY job. Mine alone. You don't go up there, you hear me?-'
'-Your job is to do exactly as you're told. Drag the coal, tend the furnace, and haul the oil blocks. Let's see if you can even manage that!-'

chapter1_end_screen.name: SYSTEM
text_list: CHAPTER 1 COMPLETED.
The isolation begins. Your mind is still sharp, but the salt is already biting into your skin.
Are you ready to face the routine?

madness_end.name: SYSTEM
text_list: NEURAL CIRCUIT FAILURE.
The mind unravels. There is nothing left but the sound of the waves.

rage_end.name: SYSTEM
text_list: RAGE UNCONTROLLED.
You lost yourself to it. The lighthouse doesn't care.
